question: Will TISZA win the most seats in the next Hungarian parliamentary election?
narrative_context: Péter Magyar's TISZA Party leading by 19-23pp across independent polls (Euronews, Bloomberg, PolitPro, Medián)
Medián Institute projects 23-point margin could deliver two-thirds parliamentary supermajority
TISZA 56% among decided voters vs Fidesz 37% (Euronews 2026-04-01), projecting 129 seats vs 100 needed for majority
PolitPro trend: TISZA 49.6% vs Fidesz 39.6%, highest differential in all polling aggregates
Election April 12 — only 10 days away, insufficient time for meaningful polling drift
Orbán's 16-year rule faces most significant electoral challenge since 2010
evidence: Euronews (2026-04-01): TISZA 56% vs Fidesz 37% among decided voters, projected 129 seats
Bloomberg (2026-04-01): Opposition poll surge shows Orbán faces election loss
Medián Institute: TISZA 58% vs 35% among likely voters, suggests supermajority
PolitPro trend: TISZA 49.6% vs Fidesz 39.6% (most current aggregate)
Cyprus Mail (2026-04-01): TISZA expands lead over Orbán; many undecided remain
US News (2026-04-01): Hungary polls show TISZA widening lead over Fidesz
counter_evidence: 26% of voters remain undecided (Cyprus Mail); late swings toward incumbent Orbán in previous elections show precedent, though current gap of 15-23pp is historic by Hungarian standards.
thesis: The market prices TISZA victory at 69.5%, but six independent polls show a 15-23 percentage point lead — widest margin in modern Hungarian politics. With 10 days to election and Fidesz unable to close such a gap in prior campaigns, our 82% estimate reflects the overwhelming polling consensus and Medián's supermajority projection. The market underprices the strength of anti-Orbán sentiment and the structural challenge facing Fidesz to recover 15+ points in a week.
x_thread: Hungary votes in 10 days. Polls show TISZA up 15-23pp over Orbán's Fidesz — a lead so wide it's nearly impossible to close in a week. Market prices TISZA at 69.5% YES. We see 82%.

Six independent polls (Euronews, Bloomberg, Medián, PolitPro) all show the same trend: TISZA 49-58% vs Fidesz 35-39%. Medián projects a two-thirds supermajority. 26% undecided, but late swings rarely exceed 10pp in modern elections.

Evidence:

• Medián Institute: TISZA 58% vs 35%, suggests supermajority territory
• PolitPro (live): TISZA 49.6% vs Fidesz 39.6% (highest differential ever recorded)
